label: LTX-2.3
notes: Speed/cost leader. Step-distilled (8 steps). ltx-pipelines built from source, not diffusers-native.

label: Wan 40-step
notes: Un-distilled 40-step default - biggest step-reduction headroom. Apache-2.0, most permissive license.

label: Hunyuan 61f
notes: CPU offload required or VAE decode OOMs (~51GB alloc). 5s clip OOMs at 832x480 - clip is half-length (2.54s). Step-distilled (8 steps).

label: Wan Turbo 4-step
notes: Step+CFG distilled via yetter-ai/Wan2.2-TI2V-5B-Turbo-Diffusers transformer swap, guidance=1.0. Warm render 8.0s vs 40-step base 46.6s on the SAME pod = 5.8x faster, same 34GB VRAM. total_seconds = warm render; cold load ~32s one-time (inflated by loading then discarding the base transformer). The widely-cited lightx2v distill LoRAs are 14B-only (hidden 5120) and do NOT fit TI2V-5B (3072) - state_dict mismatch.

label: Wan Turbo 8-step
notes: Recommended distilled recipe: 8-step (guidance=1.0) preserves motion better than 4-step. Warm render 9.6s vs 40-step base 46.6s = 4.9x. 4-step saves only 1.6s because fixed VAE-decode/text-encode overhead (~6.4s) dominates at low step counts - prefer 8-step for quality at near-equal speed.

label: Hunyuan 5s 832x480
notes: Breaks the documented 5s OOM ceiling: full 832x480x121f (5.04s) renders WITHOUT OOM via temporal VAE tiling (tile_sample_min_num_frames=16) + fp8 layerwise casting + CPU offload. Peak 68GB alloc / 78GB reserved on the 94GB card (~16GB headroom). DOUBLES clip length vs the baseline's offload-forced 2.54s. total = warm render incl tiled VAE decode.

label: Hunyuan 5s 640x384
notes: Faster 5s option: 640x384x121f renders in 81s (vs 150s at 832x480) at the SAME ~68GB alloc / 78GB reserved. Measured insight: tiled-decode peak VRAM is set by the temporal tile size + offload buffers, not spatial resolution - lower res mainly buys compute time, not memory headroom.
